title: Slow Smoky Mexican Beef Barbacoa
description: A traditional Jalisco-style dish featuring tender beef in a rich, smoky sauce, perfect for a casual brunch, this barbacoa is made with beef, spices, and poppy seeds, and is deep-fried to add crunch, and is ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly
instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat
Add the onion and cook until it is lightly browned, about 5 minutes
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant
Add the beef and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes
Add the chipotle peppers, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook for 3 hours, or until the beef is tender
After 3 hours, remove the pot from the oven and stir in the poppy seeds
Using two forks, shred the beef into bite-sized pieces
Deep-fry the shredded beef in hot oil until it is crispy, about 2 minutes
Drain the beef on paper towels and serve it with the smoky sauce spooned over the top
